statusBar.openSceneHint: Open a scene to see live statistics

welcome.pickFolderTitle: Pick parent folder for the new project
welcome.bookNameHint: Defaults to project name if left empty.
welcome.noRecentProjects: No recent projects yet. Create or open a project to get started.

settings.title: Settings
settings.fontFamilyDesc: The font used in the editor.
settings.bookSpacingDesc: Adds extra vertical space between lines for a book-like reading experience.
settings.typewriterScrollDesc: Keeps the active line at a fixed vertical position so you don't write near the bottom of the page.
settings.pageViewDesc: Renders the editor as a printed-book-style page with paper background, margins, and shadow.
settings.bookWidthDesc: Constrains the editor width to match a printed book page, so line breaks approximate the final output.
settings.bookWidthCustomDesc: Width of the text area in inches.
settings.uiLanguageDesc: Language used for the application interface.
settings.accentColorDesc: Primary highlight color used throughout the interface.
settings.scopeProjectHint: When on, this section's values are saved with the project (in its .novalist folder) and override your global settings. Open a project to enable.
settings.goalsDesc: These goals drive the status bar progress indicators for the active project.
settings.dailyWordGoalDesc: Target number of new words to write per day.
settings.projectWordGoalDesc: Target total word count for the full project.
settings.projectAuthorDesc: Author name used as default on the dashboard header and in exports.
settings.watchFilesystemDesc: Reconcile scenes and chapters you add, move, rename, or delete with a file manager while Novalist is open. Turn off for flaky network or cloud drives; changes are still picked up when you reopen the project.
settings.quoteStyleDesc: Selects the language preset for smart quotes, em-dashes, and ellipsis replacement.
settings.dialogueCorrectionDesc: Automatically corrects comma and period placement around quotation marks in dialogue, based on the selected quote style language.
settings.grammarCheckDesc: Checks your text for grammar, spelling, and style issues using LanguageTool. Requires an internet connection (or a self-hosted LanguageTool server).
settings.aiEnabledDesc: Connect to an AI provider for AI-powered analysis and chat.
settings.aiApiTokenDesc: Optional bearer token for LM Studio authentication.
settings.aiCopilotPathDesc: Path to the GitHub Copilot CLI executable. Use 'copilot' if it is on your PATH.
settings.aiSystemPromptDesc: Custom system prompt sent to the AI. Leave empty to use the default prompt. Use {{LANGUAGE}} as a placeholder for the current UI language.
settings.checkForExtensionUpdatesDesc: Automatically check for extension updates on startup
settings.githubTokenDesc: Optional token to increase API rate limits when browsing or updating extensions (60/hr → 5000/hr)
settings.diagnosticLoggingDesc: Write a technical log to a file you can send for support. It records only app events, settings, and errors — never your story text, characters, locations, scenes, or file names. You can open and read the file before sending it.

explorer.title: Novalist Explorer
explorer.addSceneTooltip: Add a scene and choose its chapter
explorer.archive.confirmDelete: Delete "{0}" forever? This cannot be undone.

entityPanel.deleteTypeConfirm: Delete entity type '{0}' and all its entities?

entityEditor.emptyState: Select an entity from the panel to edit.
entityEditor.name: Name
entityEditor.deleteConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to delete "{0}"? This cannot be undone.

context.title: NOVALIST CONTEXT
context.empty: No linked characters, locations, items, or lore detected in this chapter.
context.openSceneHint: Open a scene to view chapter and scene context.

focusPeek.aiStub: No AI findings cached. Run chapter analysis first.

wizard.resumePrompt: Found saved progress for "{0}". Resume from where you left off?

project.description: Walks you from a one-line premise to an outlined manuscript with seed cast.

projectName.title: What's the project called?
projectName.help: The display name for the project; also the folder name on disk.

bookName.title: Working title of the first book

premise.title: Premise — one sentence
premise.help: What is your story about in a single line? Try the form: A [character] wants [goal] but [obstacle].
premise.placeholder: A retired thief must rob the man who framed her.

paragraph.title: Expand the premise into a paragraph
paragraph.help: Set up the world, stakes, the inciting incident, and the rough shape of the climax.

actOne.title: Act 1 — Setup
actOne.help: What's the status quo? Who's the protagonist before they're forced to change?

actTwo.title: Act 2 — Confrontation
actTwo.help: The middle. Rising tension, complications, the protagonist tries and fails.

actThree.title: Act 3 — Resolution
actThree.help: Climax + new equilibrium. What's the cost? What changed?

chaptersPerAct.title: Roughly how many chapters per act?
chaptersPerAct.help: We'll create placeholder chapters under each act. Aim for 5–12. You can add and remove chapters freely later.

protagonists.title: Who is this story about? (protagonists)
protagonists.help: Add one or more main characters. Just names for now — develop them later in the Codex.

antagonists.title: Who stands in the way? (antagonists)
antagonists.help: Add the people, factions, or forces opposing the protagonist.

interview.description: Walks the seven psychology pillars: wound, fear, lie, want, need, secret, voice.

name.title: Name
name.help: The character's primary name. You can add aliases later.

wound.title: Wound
wound.help: A formative event from the character's past that still shapes them. Often the source of the lie they believe.

fear.title: Fear
fear.help: What does the character most fear happening to them? It's often a re-occurrence of the wound.

lie.title: Lie they believe
lie.help: The false belief about themselves or the world the character has internalised — "I'm unlovable", "strength = isolation", "the world is fair".

want.title: Want (external goal)
want.help: The thing the character is consciously chasing. Tangible, in-world, can be pointed at.

need.title: Need (internal truth)
need.help: The lesson they must learn — usually the inverse of the lie. Often realised in act three.

secret.title: Secret
secret.help: Something the character hides from other characters (and maybe themselves).

voice.title: Voice — sample lines
voice.help: A few sentences in this character's voice so their dialogue stays consistent across the manuscript.

character.surnameHelp: Family name. Leave blank if not applicable.
character.ageHelp: A literal value (e.g. "23") or descriptor (e.g. "early 30s"). Date-based ages can be set on the entity afterwards.
character.groupHelp: Family, organisation, faction — free text, used to cluster the cast.
character.descriptionHelp: A one-paragraph thumbnail — surfaces in the focus peek.

location.parentHelp: If this place sits inside another (e.g. a city inside a country).

item.originHelp: Where the item came from in-world.

map.deleteMessage: Delete map "{0}"? This cannot be undone.
map.pinEntityWatermark: Type to search characters, locations, items, lore…
map.layerAddChildTooltip: Add a child layer nested under this one.
map.isolateTooltip: Show only this image in the view so you can see what you are editing. View-only — not saved.
map.layerDeleteMessage: Delete layer "{0}" and all its images? This cannot be undone.
map.layerZoomRangeCaption: Visible zoom range — 0 = no limit on that side. Example: From=5 hides the layer until you zoom in past 5x.
map.floorMode: Floor mode (one layer at a time)
map.floorModeTooltip: Treat layers as stacked floors of a building. Only the active floor renders; others stay hidden until selected. Useful for multi-level maps where you don't want bleed-through.
map.editClipTooltip: Toggle clip-mask editing on the selected image. Drag points to reshape, double-click image to add, right-click a point to remove. Esc cancels, Enter saves.
map.toolAddImageTooltip: Add an image to the active layer.
map.toolAddPinTooltip: Pin place mode — click anywhere on the map to place a pin. Esc to cancel.
map.toolAddLabelTooltip: Label mode — click anywhere on the map to drop a text label and start typing. Esc to cancel.
map.toolZoomFitTooltip: Zoom to fit — frame all visible images.
map.toolSplineTooltip: Draw roads & rivers — pick a type, then click to lay down points. Enter finishes, Esc cancels.
map.profileBandsHint: from / to are half-width fractions: 0 = centreline, ±1 = road edge. Stack bands to build a sidewalk | curb | lanes | median cross-section.
map.splineBlend: Type-blend smoothing (0 = hard cut, 1 = full blend)
map.emptyState: No map selected. Create one on the left to get started.
map.clipHint: Drag points · Double-click image to add · Right-click point to remove · Esc to cancel
map.splineEditHint: Drag knots to move · drag outer dot for width · right-click knot to remove · Esc / Enter to finish
map.rotHandleTip: Drag to set the spline direction through this knot
map.toolTerrainTooltip: Draw terrain shapes — pick a type, then click to lay down polygon points. Enter finishes, Esc cancels.
map.terrainEditHint: Drag vertices to move · double-click the shape to add a vertex · right-click a vertex to remove · Esc / Done to finish
map.toolBuildingTooltip: Place buildings — pick a type, then click to drop. R re-rolls a new random shape, click keeps placing, Esc exits. Buildings snap to nearby roads; hold Shift to place freely.
map.bldRotateTip: Drag to rotate the building (Shift = 15° steps)
map.fpHint: Wall: click points to chain walls (Enter/Esc ends). Door/Window: click a wall or the outline. Stairs: click start then end. Right-click an element to edit or remove it.
map.fpNoFloorsWarn: Building has no floors. Add a floor first.
map.borderEditHint: Drag vertices to move · double-click the outline to add a vertex · right-click a vertex to remove · Esc / Done to finish
map.bbIdleHint: Click an element to edit. Use the ribbon to add new ones.
map.bbBuildingHint: Click to place. Shift = free angle. Right-drag + wheel = rotate.
map.bbBuildingEditHint: Drag handle to rotate. Use side panel for properties.
map.bbSelectedHint: Drag to move. Right-click for options. Delete to remove.
map.toolBorderTooltip: Map border — draw a clip boundary; everything outside it is hidden. Click again to edit an existing border.

snapshots.title: Scene snapshots
snapshots.empty: No snapshots yet. Take one before risky edits.
snapshots.confirmRestoreMessage: Replace current scene with this snapshot. Current state will be auto-snapshotted first.
snapshots.confirmDeleteMessage: This snapshot will be permanently removed.
snapshots.compareApplySelectedTooltip: For each marked line, use the snapshot version instead of the current. Current state is auto-snapshotted first.
snapshots.compareApplyHunkTooltip: Use snapshot version of this line

dashboard.subtitle: Project Dashboard
dashboard.echoDescription: Frequently repeated phrases across your manuscript. Consider varying your language where counts are high.

import.title: Import from Obsidian Plugin
import.description: Import an existing Novalist project from an Obsidian vault. Chapters, scenes, characters, locations, items, lore, images, and templates will be converted.
import.noProjectsFound: No Novalist plugin project found in this vault.

git.notARepoHint: This project is not inside a Git repository. Initialize one with 'git init' in the project folder to enable version control.
git.noChanges: Working tree clean — no changes detected.
